Sprint 3 delivered all 7 committed stories and 22 story points at 100% commitment reliability. All work was completed within the first 2 calendar days of a 14-day sprint window, driven by the paw batch pipeline running 7 stories sequentially. EPIC-3 (.NET AOT Cross-Platform Networking) is now fully complete. EPIC-7 Phase 0 POSIX signal handlers are done.

  inflate total lead time. Overnight gaps between 2026-03-07 work end and
  2026-03-09 code-review-finalize sessions account for ~60% of total lead time.
  Excluding overnight gaps, active-session efficiency is approximately 35-40%.

## Gaps Deferred to Next Sprint

No gaps from this sprint are deferred. The following are **known technical deferrals** documented in story artifacts — these are not gaps, they are tracked platform limitations:

| Item | Story | Reason | Tracking |
|------|-------|--------|---------|
| AC-VAL-1 (manual crash test on macOS/Linux) | 7-1-2 | Requires native game binary; blocked until EPIC-4 rendering migration | R8 (risk register) |
| Runtime AC-1/AC-2 on Linux x64 (.so load) | 3-3-2 | MuTests cannot run on Linux until EPIC-2 removes windows.h PCH | R6 (risk register) |
| 16 ATDD [~] items (3-3-1: 16, 3-3-2: 10) | 3-3-1, 3-3-2 | MuTests blocked by windows.h PCH until EPIC-2 migration | R6 (risk register) |

These deferrals are documented in the ATDD checklists and sprint-status.yaml risk_items. They do not block sprint completion per the HALT rules — EPIC-2 runtime removal is the prerequisite for their verification.

## Epic Impact

### EPIC-3 (.NET AOT Cross-Platform Networking) — COMPLETE

Sprint 3 delivered the final 6 EPIC-3 stories:

| Story | Status |
|-------|--------|
| 3-1-2-connection-h-crossplatform | done |
| 3-2-1-char16t-encoding | done |
| 3-3-1-macos-server-connectivity | done |
| 3-3-2-linux-server-connectivity | done |
| 3-4-1-connection-error-messaging | done |
| 3-4-2-server-connection-config | done |

EPIC-3 is now complete (all 7 stories done). Epic validation should be run next to formally close EPIC-3 and advance the critical path toward EPIC-4 (Rendering Pipeline Migration).

### EPIC-7 (Stability, Diagnostics & Quality Gates) — In Progress

Story 7-1-2 (POSIX Signal Handlers) delivered Phase 0 continuation. Remaining EPIC-7 work:
- 7-3-1 / 7-3-2 (macOS/Linux stability sessions) — depends on EPIC-2-6
- 7-4-1 (native CI runners) — depends on EPIC-4
